Package org.aavso.tools.vstar.input

Class AbstractObservationRetriever

java.lang.Object
org.aavso.tools.vstar.input.AbstractObservationRetriever
Direct Known Subclasses:
ObservationInserter, TextFormatObservationReader
public abstract class AbstractObservationRetriever extends Object
This is the abstract base class for all observation retrieval classes, irrespective of source (AAVSO standard file format, simple file format, VStar database).

      public StarInfo getStarInfo()
      Returns a StarInfo instance for the object whose observations are being loaded. Concrete subclasses may want to specialise this to add more detail.

      The StarInfo object.

      Returns the time units string (e.g. JD, HJD, BJD, ...).

      The time units string.

      Returns the brightness units string (e.g. Magnitude, Flux, ...).

      the validObservationCategoryMap

    • seriesToExcludeWhenAdditive

      public Set<SeriesType> seriesToExcludeWhenAdditive()
      Are there any series that should be excluded from addition in collectAllValidObservations() and collectAllInvalidObservations()?
      Returns:
      The set of series to be excluded from addition; may be null.

    • collectAllObservations

      public void collectAllObservations(List<ValidObservation> obs, String newSourceName) throws ObservationReadError
      Adds all of the specified observations to the current observations, including classifying them by series. This can be used for additive load operations.
      Parameters:
      obs - The list of previously existing valid observations to be added.
      newSourceName - The name of the source for new obs (in this retriever).
      Throws:
      ObservationReadError - if the observation has no date or magnitude. The caller can either propagate this exception further or add to the invalid observation list, or do whatever else it considers to be appropriate.

    • addAllInvalidObservations

      public void addAllInvalidObservations(List<InvalidObservation> obs)
      Adds all the specified invalid observations to the existing invalid observations. This can be used for additive load operations.
      Parameters:
      obs - The list of previously existing invalid observations.

    • getNumberOfRecords

      public Integer getNumberOfRecords() throws ObservationReadError
      Return number of records to be read if this observation retriever supports progress tracking (e.g. per line) or null if not.
      Returns:
      The number of records to be read or null if this cannot be determined.
      Throws:
      ObservationReadError - If an error occurs while determining the number of records.

    • incrementProgress

      public void incrementProgress()
      Increment observation retrieval progress.

    • incrementProgress

      public void incrementProgress(int steps)
      Increment observation retrieval progress by the specified number of steps.
      Parameters:
      steps - The numnber of steps.

    • collectObservation

      protected void collectObservation(ValidObservation ob) throws ObservationReadError

      Add an observation to the list of valid observations.

      This is a convenience method that adds an observation to the list of valid observations and categorises it by band/series. This method is particularly suitable for observation source plugins since it asks whether an observation satisfies the requirement that it has at least JD and magnitude values.

      Parameters:
      ob - The valid observation to be added to collections.
      Throws:
      ObservationReadError - if the observation has no date or magnitude. The caller can either propagate this exception further or add to the invalid observation list, or do whatever else it considers to be appropriate.

    • addInvalidObservation

      protected void addInvalidObservation(InvalidObservation ob)
      Add an observation to the list of invalid observations.
      Parameters:
      ob - The invalid observation to be added.

    • addValidObservation

      public void addValidObservation(ValidObservation ob)
      Adds an observation to the list of valid observations. Also, updates min/max magnitude values for the dataset.
      Parameters:
      ob - The valid observation to be added.

    • insertObservation

      public void insertObservation(ValidObservation ob, List<ValidObservation> obs)

      Insert an observation into the observation list with the post-condition that the list remains sorted by JD. This post-condition is valid iff the pre-condition that the list is already sorted before the addition is satisfied.

      An observation source plug-in developer could, if so desired, completely override this method if data is expected to be mostly out of order; in the worst case, if all elements are out of order, the cost will be O(n^2) due to the O(n) traversal being carried out n times for the number of observations inserted.

      Parameters:
      ob - The observation to be inserted.
      obs - The list into which it is to be inserted.

    • removeNegativeBytes

      protected String removeNegativeBytes(String line)
      Skip any bytes at the start of a line that have an ordinal value of less than zero, e.g. a byte-order mark sequence. This is likely to be an exceptional case so low cost when amortised over all lines.
      Parameters:
      line - The line to be processed.
      Returns:
      The line without characters whose ordinal values are negative.
